data.title: Add /dso:next zero-friction auto-router skill
data.description:
Add a /dso:next skill that reads current project state and automatically routes to the correct next action — no arguments, no confirmation dialogs, zero friction.

## Reference: get-shit-done /gsd:next

get-shit-done (47k stars) implements /gsd:next as a zero-friction auto-router that reads STATE.md and applies 8 conditional routing rules to advance automatically. It is the most-used command in the framework because it eliminates the cognitive overhead of remembering which skill to invoke next.

## Routing Rules for /dso:next

The skill reads ticket state, git state, and validation state, then routes based on conditional rules (evaluated in order, first match wins):

| # | Condition | Action |
|---|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Open P0 bugs exist | Route to /dso:fix-bug on highest-priority bug |
| 2 | Epic exists with no stories | Route to /dso:preplanning on the epic |
| 3 | Epic exists with scrutiny:pending tag | Route to /dso:brainstorm on the epic |
| 4 | Stories exist with no implementation tasks | Route to /dso:implementation-plan on the story |
| 5 | Open tasks exist ready for implementation | Route to /dso:sprint on the parent epic |
| 6 | All tasks complete, stories open | Route to story closure verification |
| 7 | All stories complete, epic open | Route to epic closure verification |
| 8 | No open work | Report "nothing to do" with suggestion to create new work |

## Design Principles

- **Zero arguments** — reads all state from tickets + git, never asks the user what to do next
- **No confirmation** — routes immediately, explaining what it chose and why in one line
- **Idempotent** — running /dso:next twice in the same state produces the same routing
- **Transparent** — always states which rule matched and what it will do before doing it
- **Composable** — can be chained: after completing a task, the user just runs /dso:next again

## State Sources

- .claude/scripts/dso ticket list — open tickets, priorities, types, parent relationships
- git status — uncommitted changes, current branch
- Validation state — whether validate.sh has passed recently
- Review state — whether a review is pending

## What This Is NOT

- Not a replacement for /dso:sprint (which orchestrates full epic execution with sub-agents)
- Not autonomous — it routes to the next skill, it doesn't execute a loop
- The user stays in control: /dso:next suggests and starts, user can interrupt or redirect
